title: Briefing 20/07/2026
summary: In Germany, Stack Infrastructure abandoned its planned €3bn hyperscale data centre outside Babenhausen, Hesse — a facility that would have sprawled over 270,000 sqm southeast of Frankfurt. Landowner Aumovio confirmed the cancellation, citing 'protracted and uncertain processes' around the planning application, after a coalition of parties on Babenhausen's city council demanded greater reliance on renewable energy, a noise-emissions cap, alternative water-cooling concepts, and a €250m guarantee certificate — demands the mayor derided as unjustified. It is the third major German withdrawal in recent months, after Google's Berlin-Brandenburg pull-out and Vantage's refusal at Gross-Gerau. In Ireland, An Coimisiún Pleanála formally confirmed it is reviewing all ten third-party appeals against the €1bn Rochfortbridge data centre campus in Co Westmeath, with a determination due by 27 October 2026. And the backlash went global: on 18 July opponents staged the first nationwide day of protest against data centres in the US — 142 protests across 42 states — while international coverage of Europe's fights, from the Aragon court challenge to Amazon's PIGA privileges to Irish grid limits, framed data centre siting as a defining political battle of the AI build-out.

sources.label: Washington Post — US national protest day

title: Briefing 13/07/2026
summary: In Ireland, plans for a €1bn data centre campus and solar farm at Rochfortbridge, Co Westmeath were stalled after 10 separate third-party appeals were lodged with An Coimisiún Pleanála against Westmeath County Council's grant of permission to Red Admiral DC Ltd; appellants cited traffic, noise, and emissions of up to 493,000 tonnes of CO2 a year. In England, The Telegraph revealed that the Minister for Investment wrote to Epping Forest District Council urging approval of the Microsoft/Nscale 'supercomputer' data centre at Loughton, which was granted last month despite objections from the town council and residents; the Local Government Association responded that councils must be treated as key partners in data centre planning. Also in England, Beaconsfield MP Joy Morrissey and Denham Parish Council objected to Mulberry's proposed hyperscale data centre on green belt land by the M40 at Denham, which has drawn 11 objections and no representations in support. In Germany, the municipality of Langenhorn (North Frisia) postponed its decision on Uniq Land's proposed 150 MW data centre after residents gave the project a hostile reception at a town hall meeting. And in Poland — the tracker's first Polish case — the group Zielona Gwiazda is organising resident opposition to DL Invest Group's planned 200 MW data centre on the former Fiat site in Bielsko-Biała, citing energy, water, and environmental concerns.

title: Coverage expanded: the Nordics and Poland
summary: We have widened our tracking to the Nordics and Poland. In Denmark, grid operator Energinet paused new data centre grid connections in March as surging demand overwhelmed Europe's cleanest power grid. In Sweden, municipalities have imposed local moratoriums on data centre development. Norway, Finland, and Poland are now under active monitoring as major growth markets.

title: Briefing 06/07/2026
summary: In the UK, Scotland became the new front line: the consultation on Apatura's 300 MW AI data centre at Larbert, Falkirk closed on 2 July with 6,719 objections against just 80 messages of support, and First Minister John Swinney said he is giving 'active consideration' to national planning guidance for hyperscale data centres. Also in Scotland, Fife Council extended the objection deadline for the proposed Auchtertool data centre after an administrative error, with a petition against the project approaching 5,000 signatures. At EU level, The Register reported that the European Commission has weakened its proposed environmental rating scheme for data centres following industry lobbying, with a revised draft going to member states. Separately, The Guardian reported that £20bn of the touted £30bn 'Stargate UK' AI investment was potential rather than committed money, and that OpenAI and its UK partner Nscale apparently never visited the key Cobalt Park data centre site in North Tyneside; OpenAI paused the project in April, citing energy costs and regulation.

title: Briefing 02/07/2026
summary: In the UK, two major new proposals are set to test local sentiment: a 1.5 GW campus in Devon from the company behind the stalled UK–Morocco power cable, and a ~150 MW facility planned on a long-stalled business park site near Norwich, Norfolk. In Ireland, debate over data centres' share of a squeezed national grid grew increasingly fractious, with the sector consuming over a fifth of national electricity. And UK campaign groups led by Global Action Plan continued organising a national coalition against 'dirty data centres'.

title: Briefing 16/03/2026
summary: In France, candidates in more than ten towns, including Marseille and Bordeaux, campaigned against new data centres or called for moratoriums in the municipal elections. In Marseille, candidate Sébastien Barles called for a moratorium, citing competition for scarce port electricity. And near Marseille, residents of Saint-Victoret moved to appeal the approved Telehouse data centre after a change of mayor.

title: Briefing 06/02/2026
summary: In Germany, local officials in Groß-Gerau, near Frankfurt, voted to reject a proposed €2.5B, 174 MW data centre after residents argued the buildings would dominate the town's skyline. In Schleswig-Holstein, a planned 150 MW facility in North Frisia met a hostile reception at a town-hall meeting, with planning permission still outstanding.

title: Briefing 23/01/2026
summary: In the UK, the government conceded in the High Court that it made a material error in approving a £1bn data centre at Iver, Buckinghamshire, without a full Environmental Impact Assessment — a win for campaign groups Foxglove and Global Action Plan. And in Ireland, the CRU's strict new grid-connection rules continue to draw criticism from both industry and opposition parties calling for a full moratorium.
